title: Lexicons as the contract surface
summary: WhatZatppa keeps com.atproto.*, app.bsky.*, and com.para.* lexicons as the canonical schema layer for records, queries, and views.

title: PDS and AppView service split
summary: The backend language reflects the WhatZatppa separation between personal data hosting, appview APIs, identity, repo, and xrpc service layers.

title: Record-to-view indexing pipeline
summary: Raw repo commits are transformed into queryable views through an indexing layer. Posts become feed items, votes become cabildeo aggregates, and actor activity becomes profile stats.

title: Views over record scans
summary: All list and detail queries serve from pre-materialized indexed views rather than scanning raw records. This keeps civic aggregates fast and consistent at scale.

title: Civic view materialization
summary: Cabildeo vote totals, position counts, delegation summaries, and governance rosters are maintained by the indexer and served as fields in com.para.civic.defs and com.para.community.defs views.

title: Actor stats materialization
summary: Influence scores, community contributions, and active community lists are computed from indexed activity and served through com.para.actor.getProfileStats.

title: Highlight annotation indexing
summary: Public and private highlight ranges over subject records are indexed into browseable views with subject, creator, community, and state filters.

title: Metadata overlays on posts
summary: com.para.social.postMeta attaches civic classification (policy, matter, meme) and vote scores to existing posts without modifying the base record.

title: Stable core, experimental edges
summary: Post and status records are stable. Views and queries are experimental because their field sets evolve as product surfaces mature.
